title: Preventing Unjust Red Flag Laws Act of 2025
short_title: Preventing Unjust Red Flag Laws Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill stops the federal government from using taxpayer money to support red flag laws. Red flag laws allow courts to order the removal of guns from people who might be dangerous to themselves or others. The bill says these laws do not give people fair treatment in court before taking their guns away. Under this bill, no federal money can be used to create, enforce, or help states and local governments use these types of gun removal laws.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Preventing Unjust Red Flag Laws Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Funding for Implementation and Enforcement of Red Flag Laws or Rules
sections.text: The federal government cannot use any of its money to carry out or enforce red flag laws. The federal government also cannot give money to state, local, tribal, or territorial governments to help them carry out or enforce red flag laws. A red flag law is a court order that allows officials to temporarily take away someone's firearms based on the risk they might harm themselves or others. These orders are given before the gun owner gets a chance to defend themselves in court, which means they happen without due process (the legal right to have a fair hearing before losing something important).
